description: Add ability to create complete copies of skills into project vaults alongside existing symlink management for portability and offline use

userStories.title: Copy skill operations module
userStories.description: As a developer, I need backend operations for copying skills, detecting copies, computing drift, and removing copies so the UI can use them.
acceptanceCriteria: copy_skill(src: Path, dst: Path) -> OperationResult performs shutil.copytree(src, dst) with parent directory creation via dst.parent.mkdir(parents=True, exist_ok=True)
remove_copy(dst: Path) -> OperationResult performs shutil.rmtree(dst) with OSError handling
is_copy(path: Path) -> bool returns True if path exists and is a directory and is not a symlink

compute_drift(source_skill: Path, copied_skill: Path) -> bool returns True if file content SHA-256 hashes differ between source and copy (walks both trees, compares sorted file lists and per-file hashes)

Unit tests cover: successful copy, copy to existing path fails, remove copy, is_copy vs is_symlink vs missing, drift detection with identical trees, drift detection with changed file, drift detection with added/removed file
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Replace matrix checkboxes with split link/copy icons
userStories.description: As a user, I want each matrix cell to show two small icons (link and copy) instead of a single checkbox so I can choose how to place each skill.
acceptanceCriteria: Each matrix cell renders two small icons side-by-side: Material Icon 'link' and 'content_copy'
Empty cell: both icons are dimmed/neutral, both clickable
Symlinked cell (os.path.islink is True): link icon is highlighted in blue/primary color, copy icon is grayed out and disabled (pointer-events: none)
Copied cell (os.path.exists and not os.path.islink): copy icon is highlighted in green, link icon is grayed out and disabled
Cell container has min-width of 140px to accommodate both icons
Matrix title changed from 'Symlink Matrix' to 'Skill Matrix'
Typecheck passes
Verify in browser using dev-browser skill

userStories.title: Wire link icon to create and remove symlinks
userStories.description: As a user, I want clicking the link icon to create or remove a symlink, preserving the existing symlink behavior.
acceptanceCriteria: Clicking the link icon on an empty cell creates a symlink (same as existing checkbox on-check behavior)
Link icon becomes highlighted, copy icon becomes disabled
Clicking the highlighted link icon removes the symlink (same as existing checkbox on-uncheck behavior)
Cell returns to empty state with both icons enabled
Conflict detection still triggers when creating a symlink where another source's skill exists (existing find_owning_source behavior preserved)
Error notifications shown on failure, icon state reverts
Typecheck passes
Verify in browser using dev-browser skill

userStories.title: Wire copy icon to create and remove copies
userStories.description: As a user, I want clicking the copy icon to create a full copy of the skill folder, and clicking it again to remove the copy.
acceptanceCriteria: Clicking the copy icon on an empty cell calls copy_skill(source_skill_path, target_dir/skill_name)
Copy icon becomes highlighted in green, link icon becomes disabled
Clicking the highlighted copy icon calls remove_copy(target_dir/skill_name)
Cell returns to empty state with both icons enabled
Error notifications shown on failure, icon state reverts
Disabled icons for missing project columns still work (both icons disabled with tooltip)
Typecheck passes
Verify in browser using dev-browser skill

userStories.title: Detect and display drifted copies after git pull
userStories.description: As a user, I want to see which copied skills are outdated after updating a source so I know to re-copy them.
acceptanceCriteria: After git pull completes (Update or Update All), for each updated source: find all copied skills (is_copy returns True) across all target directories
For each found copy, call compute_drift(source_skill_path, copy_path)
If drifted: the copy icon in the matrix shows an amber dot/badge overlay
Hovering the amber badge shows tooltip: 'Source has been updated since this copy was made'
Drift state is stored in a runtime dict (not persisted to config), keyed by target_dir/skill_name
Drift badge clears when the copy is removed (cell returns to empty)
Non-updated sources are not drift-checked (optimization)
Typecheck passes
Verify in browser using dev-browser skill

userStories.title: Source removal leaves copies untouched
userStories.description: As a user, I want copies to survive when I remove a skill source since copies are independent snapshots.
acceptanceCriteria: find_source_symlinks only returns actual symlinks (os.path.islink check), not copied directories
Source removal confirmation dialog only lists symlinks that will be removed, not copies
After source removal, copied skill folders remain in their project vaults
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Update source group header counts for copies
userStories.description: As a user, I want the source group headers in the matrix to reflect both linked and copied skills.
acceptanceCriteria: Source group header shows count as 'X/Y linked, Z copied' instead of just 'X/Y linked'
Linked count only counts symlinks (os.path.islink)
Copied count only counts copies (os.path.exists and not os.path.islink)
If copied count is 0, only 'X/Y linked' is shown (no change from current behavior)
Typecheck passes
Verify in browser using dev-browser skill
